findings.description: Stale-lock recovery renames the lock path without re-validating that the on-disk lock still matches the stale owner that was read. Two recoverers that both observed the same dead lock can interleave as: A renames+creates, then B renames A's newly created lock away (POSIX rename replaces a file destination) and creates its own lock. Both callers then enter withPublisherLock critical sections concurrently.
findings.failure_mode: Concurrent compareAndSwapRoot / appendAuditEvent / appendRevocation mutations corrupt generation pointers, audit/revocation contiguity, or produce split-brain publisher writes despite the lock.
findings.suggested_fix: Make recovery compare-and-swap: after reading the stale owner, only rename/unlink if the current lock bytes still match that owner (or use an O_EXCL recovery claim). Treat mismatch/ENOENT as contention and retry from a fresh acquire, never assume rename of a previously observed stale lock is still valid.

findings.description: putObjectIfAbsent is not under the publisher lock and treats existsSync as the only race gate before durableCreate(..., "wx"). A concurrent second put of the same digest loses on EEXIST and surfaces a hard filesystem failure instead of the idempotent success path used when the object already exists.
findings.failure_mode: Parallel publishers/reconciler puts of identical content fail closed with a non-retryable registry error even though the object was correctly created, breaking if-absent semantics and stalling activation.
findings.suggested_fix: On EEXIST from durableCreate, re-read and succeed iff sha256 matches digest (else integrity-fail). Mirror the in-memory store's digest-equal idempotency.

findings.description: Consumer state only tracks greenTargets. ingest deletes a revoked target from the green cache, but cacheGreen unconditionally re-adds any targetId and isGreen only checks greenTargets membership. No durable revoked-target set is consulted.
findings.failure_mode: After a verified emergency revocation, any later cacheGreen(target) makes isGreen(target) true again, re-authorizing a revoked key/artifact.
findings.suggested_fix: Persist revoked target IDs (or deny-list) in consumer state on ingest/repair; make cacheGreen/isGreen refuse or ignore targets present in that set.

findings.cross_file_justification: openCertifiedFilesystemTruthRegistryStore (filesystem-registry-store.ts) treats evaluateFilesystemCertification success as deployment qualification authority, while the schema distinguishes FIXTURE_VALID vs STAGED_VALID and the fixture opener is documented as having no qualification authority.
findings.description: evaluateFilesystemCertification never requires validity === "STAGED_VALID" (or rejects FIXTURE_VALID). A FIXTURE_VALID certificate with status QUALIFIED and passing probe/dedicated-runner fields is accepted, then openCertifiedFilesystemTruthRegistryStore opens the production-shaped adapter.
findings.failure_mode: Fixture/local evidence can authorize the certified filesystem registry path, violating fail-closed deployment qualification.
findings.suggested_fix: Fail closed unless validity is STAGED_VALID (and keep FIXTURE_VALID only for explicitly non-certified test helpers).

findings.cross_file_justification: registry-store.ts:makeInMemoryTruthRegistryStore.appendRevocation enforces unique event_id for the same TruthRegistryStoreService contract; the filesystem adapter omits that check.
findings.description: appendRevocation only checks prior-sequence file existence before durableCreate. Duplicate event_id values at different sequences are accepted on disk.
findings.failure_mode: Filesystem-backed registries can store duplicate revocation identities that the in-memory reference rejects, breaking cross-backend integrity equivalence and allowing conflicting revoke records for the same event_id.
findings.suggested_fix: Before create, scan existing revocation records (or maintain an event_id index) and fail if event_id already exists, matching the in-memory store.

findings.description: Body/header sequence and epoch equality uses Number(record.sequence/epoch) without a Number.isSafeInteger guard. DecimalUint64 values above MAX_SAFE_INTEGER coerce, so distinct body integers can compare equal to header numbers.
findings.failure_mode: Exact envelope↔body sequence/epoch binding required by the trust-envelope contract can pass for non-identical large decimals, accepting a mismatched stream position vs revocation record.
findings.suggested_fix: Compare with BigInt (or decimal-string equality) and reject values outside the envelope header's representable domain; align with emitEmergencyRevocationEnvelope's safe-integer check.

findings.description: Lock files are JSON.parse'd as LockOwner without schema validation. If created_at_ms is missing/non-numeric, oldEnough becomes false and the lock is treated as live forever even when the pid is dead.
findings.failure_mode: A corrupt publisher.lock permanently returns STORE_UNAVAILABLE until manual deletion.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate lock shape; on invalid lock contents, treat as reclaimable stale/corrupt and replace under exclusive create.
